mcqs.question: Who is credited with founding Extreme Programming?
mcqs.explanation: Kent Beck developed Extreme Programming in 1996 while working on the Chrysler Comprehensive Compensation System project.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four core values of XP?
mcqs.explanation: The four core values of XP are Communication, Simplicity, Feedback, and Courage. Documentation is not considered a core value.

mcqs.question: In Pair Programming, who is responsible for writing the code?
mcqs.explanation: In Pair Programming, the Driver is the one actively writing code while the Navigator reviews and provides feedback.

mcqs.question: What is the correct order of the TDD cycle?
mcqs.explanation: TDD follows Red (write failing test), Green (write minimal code to pass), and Refactor (improve code while keeping tests passing).

mcqs.question: What does the Planning Game in XP primarily determine?
mcqs.explanation: The Planning Game is a collaborative effort between customers and developers to determine what features to build and when they will be delivered.

mcqs.question: Which XP practice involves improving code structure without changing behavior?
mcqs.explanation: Refactoring is the practice of continuously improving code structure, design, and readability without altering its external behavior.

mcqs.question: What is the recommended frequency for Continuous Integration?
mcqs.explanation: In XP, code should be integrated multiple times per day to detect integration issues early and maintain a working build.

mcqs.question: What does the Metaphor practice in XP provide?

options.text: A shared story describing system behavior

mcqs.correctAnswer: A shared story describing system behavior
mcqs.explanation: The Metaphor provides a simple, shared story or description that helps the whole team understand how the system works.

mcqs.question: What is Collective Code Ownership?

options.text: Only senior developers can modify code
Any developer can modify any code

mcqs.correctAnswer: Any developer can modify any code
mcqs.explanation: Collective Code Ownership means any team member can modify any part of the codebase, promoting shared responsibility.

mcqs.question: How many hours per week does Sustainable Pace recommend?
mcqs.explanation: Sustainable Pace (40-hour week) emphasizes consistent, sustainable work hours to maintain productivity and avoid burnout.

mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of User Stories in XP?
mcqs.explanation: User Stories are brief descriptions of features written from the customer's perspective, focusing on user needs and value.

mcqs.question: Which practice requires a customer to be physically present with the development team?
mcqs.explanation: On-site Customer practice requires a real customer or representative to work closely with the development team daily.

mcqs.question: What is the XP approach to code documentation?
mcqs.explanation: XP values working code over comprehensive documentation, preferring simple, essential documentation that adds value.

mcqs.question: In TDD, what does the 'Red' phase mean?
mcqs.explanation: In the Red phase, developers write a test that fails because the functionality doesn't exist yet.

mcqs.question: What is the primary benefit of Continuous Integration?
mcqs.explanation: Continuous Integration helps detect integration problems early by frequently merging code and running automated tests.

mcqs.question: Which of these is a role in XP?
mcqs.explanation: XP has four key roles: Programmer, Customer, Tester, and Coach. Project Manager and Scrum Master are not XP-specific roles.

mcqs.question: What is the Simple Design principle in XP?
mcqs.passage: Based on the principle that you aren't going to need it

options.text: Build only what is needed now

mcqs.correctAnswer: Build only what is needed now
mcqs.explanation: Simple Design emphasizes building only what is required at the moment, avoiding over-engineering (YAGNI principle).

mcqs.question: What is an Acceptance Test in XP?

options.text: Test written by programmers for code units
Test written by customers to verify user stories

mcqs.correctAnswer: Test written by customers to verify user stories
mcqs.explanation: Acceptance Tests are written by customers to verify that the functionality described in user stories works correctly.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of Coding Standards in XP?
mcqs.explanation: Coding Standards ensure all team members follow consistent formatting and style guidelines, making code easier to read and maintain.

mcqs.question: XP is considered which type of methodology?
mcqs.explanation: XP is one of the original Agile methodologies, focusing on iterative development, customer collaboration, and adaptive planning.
